deployMaintenanceMessage: All is ok, we are upgrading, will be available in ~ 2 mins
pageTitle: Open EMS — Power flow | smart EMS for microgrids
inverterSourcesNotConfigured: Not configured (set DEYE_* and/or HUAWEI_* on server)
huaweiNorthboundRateLimited: Plant list temporarily unavailable (FusionSolar rate limit). Retry in a few minutes; avoid Postman or other tools using the same Northbound user.
huaweiPowerFlowRateLimitedHint: FusionSolar rate limit — live power refreshes every ~10 min
huaweiAuthFailedHint: FusionSolar login failed (check HUAWEI_USER_NAME and HUAWEI_SYSTEM_CODE on the server).
ubetterAuthFailedHint: Ubetter EMS login failed (check UBETTER_PASSWORD on the server).
dischargeGoAria: Run SoC discharge toward the selected floor (selling first, then zero export to CT). May take many minutes.
dischargeGoDisabledTooltip: Select a lower target SoC or charge above {{target}}%.
dischargeSocDeltaAria: Target SoC (%) after discharge. Saved for peak DAM auto-discharge and as the manual discharge default.
dischargeSoc2Hint: Selling-first until SoC reaches the selected floor, then zero export to CT (rewrites Deye TOU template). May take many minutes.
dischargeSoc2Partial: {{from}}% → {{to}}%. Target SoC was not reached within the server time limit; zero export to CT restored.
dischargeSoc2PartialFlat: {{from}}% → {{to}}%. Cloud SoC did not show a change before the time limit; zero export to CT was restored. The inverter may still be discharging—reported SoC often lags.
dischargeConfirmNoSoc: Current battery SoC is not available. Wait for the value to load, then try again.
dischargeConfirmInsufficientSoc: Cannot reach {{target}}% SoC: current level ({{current}}%) is already at or below that target.
chargeGoAria: Run grid-biased charge toward higher SoC (may take many minutes).
chargeSocDeltaAria: Target SoC increase: 2, 10, 20, 50, or 100 percentage points (capped at 100%). Saved for this inverter.
chargeSoc2Hint: Raises battery SoC via dynamicControl (same TOU overwrite as discharge). May take many minutes.
chargeSoc2Partial: {{from}}% → {{to}}%. Target SoC was not reached within the server time limit; zero export to CT restored.
chargeSoc2PartialFlat: {{from}}% → {{to}}%. Cloud SoC did not show a change before the time limit; zero export to CT was restored. Charging may still be in progress—reported SoC often lags.
deyeCommandWaiting: Waiting for Deye. This may take several minutes…
deyeCommandContinuesBackground: The inverter continues until the target SoC is reached, then restores zero export to CT.
ubetterCommandContinuesBackground: The ESS continues until the target SoC is reached (manual run-strategy).
ubetterChargePartial: {{from}}% → {{to}}%. Manual charge accepted by Ubetter cloud.
ubetterDischargePartial: {{from}}% → {{to}}%. Manual discharge accepted by Ubetter cloud.
deyeWritePinTitlePeak: Enter PIN to change peak DAM discharge
deyeWritePinTitleLow: Enter PIN to change low DAM charge
deyeWritePinTitlePeakPct: Enter PIN to save discharge depth
deyeWritePinTitleLowPct: Enter PIN to save charge depth
deyeWritePinTitleSelfConsumption: Enter PIN to change self consumption
deyeWritePinTitleSelfConsumptionAutoDam: Enter PIN to change self consumption
deyeWritePinTitleNightCharge: Enter PIN to change night charge (23:00–07:00)
deyeWritePinTitleSmartLoad: Enter PIN to change smart-load automation
deyeRemoteWriteNeedsPin: Remote control is off for this inverter. Add a PIN suffix in the Deye Cloud plant or inverter name (e.g. "My site pin1234"), then reload the list.
chargeConfirmNoSoc: Current battery SoC is not available. Wait for the value to load, then try again.
chargeConfirmNoHeadroom: Not enough room to add that much SoC (already near 100%).
lowDamChargeToggleAria: Server finds today’s lowest DAM hour, scans ±3 h around it, and starts charge at the cheapest 3 consecutive hours; depth is the dropdown value (+10% … +100%). One successful run per day per inverter; retries after failure.
lowDamChargeToggleHint: Stored on the server (Flyway V9). Low-DAM auto uses the same SoC rise % as the dropdown.
lowDamChargeSaveError: Could not save low-DAM charge preference
nightChargeToggleAria: When enabled, the server charges the battery during the Kyiv night window (23:00–07:00), enables self-consumption during the day (07:00–22:59) so the battery can cover load, and turns off peak DAM discharge and low DAM charge. Depth uses the charge SoC dropdown value. One successful charge per night per inverter.
nightChargeToggleHint: Stored on the server (Flyway V23). Uses Europe/Kyiv local time.
nightChargeSaveError: Could not save night charge preference
peakDamDischargeToggleAria: When enabled, the server starts a sell-first discharge in the Kyiv clock hour when today’s DAM price is highest. Discharge stops at {{tillSoc}} (selector range 0–95%). One successful run per day per inverter at that hour; retries if the run fails.
peakDamDischargeToggleHint: The battery will sell energy to the grid in today’s peak DAM hour (Kyiv time) until {{tillSoc}}.
peakDamDischargeSaveError: Could not save peak DAM preference
selfConsumptionToggleAria: When on, the server enables self-consumption while the current-hour DAM (UAH/kWh) is above reference battery LCOE ({{lcoe}}). Inverter: ZERO_EXPORT_TO_CT using the same floor as {{tillSoc}} from the selector. Peak DAM uses remaining SoC.
selfConsumptionToggleHint: Auto when spot DAM is above battery LCOE ({{lcoe}}). Uses {{tillSoc}} from the selector.
selfConsumptionAutoDamToggleAria: When enabled, the server turns self-consumption on when the current Kyiv hour DAM price (UAH/kWh) is above the reference battery LCOE (NBU × POWER_FLOW_*), and off when it is not. Night charge disables this rule. Manual self-consumption is locked while auto is on.
selfConsumptionAutoDamToggleHint: Same LCOE formula as GET /api/power-flow/reference-lcoe. Scheduler: DEYE_SELF_CONSUMPTION_AUTO_DAM_* env.
selfConsumptionAutoDamSaveError: Could not save DAM vs LCOE auto preference
smartLoadToggleAria: Compares PV and smart load power; keeps max load from PV when solar is enough.
smartLoadToggleHint: Compares PV and smart load power; keeps max load from PV.
genOnGridAlwaysOnToggleAria: Debug: Gen port On Grid always on on the inverter.
genOnGridAlwaysOnToggleHint: Debug: writes Gen port On Grid always on directly to Deye.
genOnGridAlwaysOnSaveError: Could not change On Grid always on
deyeWritePinTitleGenOnGrid: Enter PIN to change On Grid always on (Gen port)
genPortSettingsUnderDevelopmentHint: Smart load and On grid always on are under development and disabled for now.
qrAsideAria: B12 — uncrewed systems unit, b12.army (opens in new tab)
openSourceGithubAria: Open EMS source code on GitHub (opens in new tab)
sharePageAria: Share link to this page (station, language, inverter)
pageShareQrAsideAria: QR code — link to this page (station, language, inverter)
openEmsKioskAria: Present — power flow and QR code
openEmsKioskFleetGenConsAria: Fleet bar chart — generation and consumption per inverter today
powerFlowLandingPeakDamSessionLabel: Peak DAM export — kWh to grid (all sessions)
powerFlowLandingPeakDamSessionHint: Total kWh exported during every recorded peak auto-discharge session for this inverter (each run from start until zero-export restore), summed over all time.
powerFlowLandingPeakDamSessionSubtitleMi: Stopped before target SoC (time limit or flat cloud SoC)
powerFlowLandingManualDischargeLabel: Manual discharge — kWh to grid (all sessions)
powerFlowLandingManualDischargeHint: Total kWh exported during every recorded UI/API manual discharge session for this inverter (each run until zero-export restore), summed over all time.
powerFlowLandingManualDischargeSubtitleM: Stopped before target SoC (time limit or flat cloud SoC)
powerFlowLandingGridBalancingCounterAria: {{month}} {{pct}}% grid balancing — import avoided in peak DAM hour ±1 h
powerFlowLandingGridBalancingHint: Current Kyiv month: average daily grid-balancing score (%). 100% when grid import is zero in the peak DAM hour and the hour before/after; otherwise 100×(1 − peak-window import ÷ daily import). Click for monthly history.
powerFlowLandingGridBalancingHintFleet: Fleet: same grid-balancing score (%) across all Deye inverters. Peak hour = highest DAM price of the day (earlier hour on tie). Click for monthly history.
powerFlowLandingGridBalancingClickHint: Click the value above for a monthly bar chart
powerFlowLandingTariffVsUkraineMoreAfter: higher than the average tariff in Ukraine
powerFlowLandingTariffVsUkraineLessAfter: lower than the average tariff in Ukraine
powerFlowLandingTariffVsUkraineEqual: Your tariff matches the average tariff in Ukraine
powerFlowGridBalancingChartTooltipPeakIm: Import in peak DAM ±1 h: {{kwh}} kWh
powerFlowGridBalancingChartEmpty: No grid-balancing score for this period.
powerFlowGridBalancingChartError: Could not load grid balancing chart.
powerFlowLandingMonthlyRatesHint: Retail tariff for the current Kyiv month: DAM average + distribution + VAT. Click to open monthly history.
powerFlowLandingMonthlyRatesHintDevice: Retail tariff for this inverter: grid import weighted by hourly DAM (+ distribution + VAT). Click for monthly history.
powerFlowLandingMonthlyRatesHintEvPorts: Retail tariff for selected EV chargers: charging power from DB weighted by hourly DAM (+ distribution + VAT). Click for monthly history.
powerFlowLandingMonthlyRatesClickHint: Click the value above for a monthly bar chart
powerFlowMonthlyRatesChartTitleDevice: Monthly retail tariff — this inverter (import × DAM + distribution + VAT)
powerFlowMonthlyRatesChartTooltipImport: Grid import matched to DAM: {{kwh}} kWh
powerFlowMonthlyRatesChartEmpty: No DAM data for this period.
powerFlowMonthlyRatesChartError: Could not load monthly tariff chart.
powerFlowLandingExportTotalHint: Total grid export (kWh): sum of every stored 5‑min sample for this inverter — not the peak-DAM or manual session counters.
powerFlowLandingExportTotalHintFleet: Total grid export (kWh): sum of all samples for all inverters — not peak-DAM or manual session counters.
powerFlowLandingPeakDamSessionHintFleet: Sum of all peak DAM auto-discharge session exports (every inverter, all time).
powerFlowLandingManualDischargeHintFleet: Sum of all manual UI/API discharge session exports (every inverter, all time).
powerFlowLandingExportMetricLostSolar7dH: Total estimated clipped PV (kWh) over every Kyiv day that has inverter samples in the database — same clear-sky / first 100% SoC model as the DAM chart’s «Lost solar this day», using plant GPS when available.
Per-inverter metric only (not shown for fleet).
powerFlowLandingArbitrageHint: UAH: each Kyiv calendar hour sums import and export kWh from 5‑min samples (W/12000); that hour adds (export − import) × DAM UAH/kWh when OREE has a price. Total = sum of those hourly amounts over all history — same as summing each day’s «Arbitrage revenue for this day» on the DAM chart.
powerFlowLandingArbitrageHintFleet: Fleet: same hourly aggregation across all inverters, then summed over all days.
powerFlowLandingTariffLineLead: Tariff this {{month}} will be ~ {{rate}} UAH/kWh incl. distribution and VAT (
powerFlowLandingTariffPartial: Tariff this {{month}} will be ~ {{rate}} UAH/kWh incl. distribution and VAT
powerFlowLandingDamTariffLine: DAM Tariff this {{month}} for this inverter = {{rate}} UAH/kWh incl. distribution and VAT
powerFlowLandingTariffUnavailable: DAM tariff comparison is unavailable (OREE or DB data missing).
powerFlowExportHourlyChartTitleTotal: Hourly grid export — last {{days}} Kyiv days (hours with export only)
powerFlowExportHourlyChartTitlePeak: Peak automatic export by hour — last {{days}} Kyiv days (peak-DAM session buckets only)
powerFlowExportHourlyChartTitleManual: Manual discharge export by hour — last {{days}} Kyiv days (session buckets only)
powerFlowExportHourlyChartTitleArbitrage: Hourly export revenue at DAM — last {{days}} Kyiv days (hours with export only)
powerFlowLostSolarHourlyChartTitle: Hourly lost solar — last {{days}} Kyiv days (clipped PV hours only)
powerFlowLostSolarHourlyChartEmpty: No estimated clipped PV in this period.
powerFlowPeakHourlyChartEmpty: No grid export in this period.
solarInsolationDayGraphAria: Hourly insolation forecast for today at the plant location
solarInsolationTomorrowGraphAria: Hourly insolation forecast for tomorrow at the plant location
solarInsolationNoCoords: Set plant location in Deye Cloud for forecast
gridDamTariffNodeTitle: OREE DAM UAH/kWh for the current Kyiv clock hour today when hourly data exists; otherwise the current-month average (MTD) from landing totals.
gridHiddenByInverter: Aggregate grid is hidden while an inverter is selected
hubBrandLinkAria: 220 km — official site (opens in new tab)
hubPartnerCarouselAria: {name} — partner promotion. Click to pause rotation, click again to resume, double-click to open website.
hubPartnerPausedAria: {name} — rotation paused. Click to resume, double-click to open website.
evHiddenByInverter: Aggregate EV charging is hidden while an inverter is selected
lcoeBatteryMetaTitle: Illustrative LiFePO4 LCOE (UAH/kWh): reference $/kWh × BOS ÷ (usable DOD × equivalent cycles) at NBU USD→UAH. Tune POWER_FLOW_REF_LIFEPO4_USD_PER_KWH, POWER_FLOW_BATTERY_BOP_MULT, POWER_FLOW_BATTERY_USABLE_DOD, POWER_FLOW_BATTERY_EQUIV_CYCLES. Not a site quote.
lcoeSolarMetaTitle: Illustrative tier-1 PV LCOE (UAH/kWh): installed $/W at NBU USD→UAH ÷ (yield kWh/kWp·year × years). Tune POWER_FLOW_REF_PV_USD_PER_W, POWER_FLOW_PV_LIFE_YEARS, POWER_FLOW_PV_YIELD_KWH_PER_KW_YEAR. Not a site quote.
damPageTitle: Open EMS — DAM chart | arbitrage & ML forecast
damEntsoeNotConfigured: ENTSOE_SECURITY_TOKEN is not set — run sync on a server with the token, or prices stay empty.
damLegendAriaToggleUaEntsoe: Show or hide UA (ENTSO-E Transparency) EUR/kWh line
damHuaweiDbSamplesHint: No Huawei power samples in the database for this Kyiv calendar day yet. The server saves them periodically when HUAWEI_* is configured, or you can refresh now.
damEvPortsDbSamplesHint: No EV port power samples in the database for this Kyiv calendar day yet. The server saves them every 5 minutes, or you can refresh now.
damEnergyTotalsGridAria: Grid import and export totals and arbitrage revenue for the selected calendar day
damEnergyTotalsPvLoadAria: Generation, consumption, and lost solar totals for the selected calendar day
damEnergyArbitrageRevenueSub: Per hour: +export kWh × DAM UAH/kWh − import kWh × DAM UAH/kWh; day total = sum of hours (same as landing arbitrage).
damEnergyDamUahUnavailable: DAM in UAH/kWh unavailable (missing price or EUR/UAH rate)
damEnergyDamPartialHoursNote: DAM totals include only hours with both grid energy and DAM price; kWh above uses all hours with grid data.
damOreeNotConfigured: OREE_API_KEY is not set — DAM line will be empty until prices are synced on a server with the key.
damSyncNote: DAM prices were just synced from OREE for this view.
damLazyOreeExhausted: On-demand OREE sync for tomorrow reached the server limit. DAM uses the database only until the next scheduled sync or POST /api/dam/sync.
damOreeWebsiteAria: Opens the OREE website in a new tab
damOreeEmbedIframeTitle: OREE — official day-ahead market (DAM) on oree.com.ua

seoMetaDescription: Open EMS — smart energy management for microgrids in Ukraine: solar, BESS, and EV charging. Automated charge/discharge scenarios, DAM/IDM arbitrage (OREE), ML solar and price forecasts, Modbus/MQTT/OPC UA, island mode, grid balancing. Deye & Huawei monitoring. Self-hosted, open-source.
seoMetaKeywords: smart energy management system, microgrid EMS, BESS monitoring, solar plant monitoring, ML solar generation forecast, ML day-ahead price forecast, electricity arbitrage, DAM IDM, active consumer, island mode, Modbus MQTT OPC UA, Open EMS, EMS Ukraine, OREE, grid balancing, Deye, Huawei FusionSolar, smart charging, 220-km, support Ukraine energy system, compare your consumption with neighbor, compare your tariff with neighbor, підтримай енергосистему України, порівняй своє споживання з сусідом, порівняй свій тариф з сусідом
seoOgTitle: Open EMS — smart EMS for microgrids | DAM, IDM, BESS, solar
damSeoMetaDescription: Open EMS DAM chart: OREE day-ahead prices, volumes, ENTSO-E overlay, ML forecast. DAM/IDM arbitrage and grid balancing for solar and BESS.
damSeoOgTitle: Open EMS — DAM chart | arbitrage & ML price forecast
landingPageTitle: Open EMS — smart energy management | microgrid, BESS, DAM/IDM
landingSeoMetaDescription: Open EMS — open-source smart energy management for microgrids in Ukraine: solar, BESS, EV charging. Automated charge/discharge, DAM/IDM arbitrage (OREE), ML forecasts, Modbus/MQTT/OPC UA, island mode. Self-hosted.
landingSeoOgTitle: Open EMS — smart EMS for microgrids | DAM, BESS, solar
landingHeroOpenText: Open EMS automatically manages your microgrid — solar, BESS, EV charging, and DAM/IDM arbitrage.
landingHeroSecureText: The source code is open — you can deploy it on your own server, isolated from the public internet.
landingHowLead: Open EMS connects to your inverter via REST API or Modbus and runs any scenario: home automation, industrial energy management, tariff and demand management at EV charging hubs, self-consumption, and DAM/IDM arbitrage.
landingIntegrationImageAlt: BESS integration analogy: REST API is simple and secure at the steering wheel; Modbus is expensive and complex under the hood
landingIntegrationRestText: Simple and secure — like driving from the cabin. Connect through vendor cloud APIs (Deye Cloud, Huawei FusionSolar): no opening the hood at every site, HTTPS, and ready-made Open EMS scenarios.
landingIntegrationModbusText: Expensive and complex — like working under the hood on every engine. Direct Modbus RTU/TCP register access: higher install and commissioning cost, but less dependence on the Chinese vendor's cloud API.
landingHowExample: The system decides when to charge batteries, when to use stored energy, and when to sell surplus to the day-ahead market.
landingScenario1Title: Earn with savings as a bonus
landingScenario1Text: Primary goal is maximum market revenue; savings on own consumption are an extra benefit.
landingScenario2Text: Lower bills: self-consumption from the battery at peak hours, charge during cheap DAM periods.
landingScenario3Text: Cover your own needs first, then sell excess capacity on DAM/IDM.
landingScenario4Text: Buy low, sell high — using OREE price forecasts and ML models.
landingModulesTitle: From solar monitoring to full EMS
landingModulesLead: Four tiers — start where you need and scale without migrations.
landingModuleSolarDesc: For solar plant owners who want forecasted generation and transparent revenue.
landingModuleActiveGridDesc: For small BESS and BESS+solar used mainly for own consumption.
landingModuleArbitrageDesc: For BESS aimed at commercial arbitrage on day-ahead and intraday markets.
landingModuleArbitrageF1: Peak DAM discharge, low DAM charge
landingModuleMicroGridDesc: For complex energy systems with generation, load, balancing, and island mode.
landingAudience1Text: Have solar and BESS and want to monetize surplus? Open EMS analyzes DAM/IDM, charges at low prices, and sells in peak periods.
landingAudience2Text: Instead of a fixed green tariff — sell at market prices with automated decisions on when to sell or store.
landingAudience2B1: Market prices instead of fixed FIT
landingAudience3Text: Multiple generation and load sources in one system — optimal energy source every moment based on prices and schedule.
landingConnectLead: Four steps — from request to running your microgrid on Open EMS.
landingStep1Text: Leave a request and describe equipment: solar, BESS, EV ports, Deye or Huawei inverters.
landingStep2Text: Based on your equipment and goals, we show revenue forecast and control scenarios.
landingStep3Text: Industrial protocol integration and UI tailored to your needs.
landingStep4Text: Run Open EMS self-hosted on your server or in the cloud. Open source on GitHub.
landingBenefitsLead: Engineering for Ukraine's electricity market and open architecture.
landingBenefit1Text: Charge/discharge scenarios, self-consumption, peak DAM and night charge — without manual intervention.
landingBenefit2Text: Modbus, MQTT, OPC UA. Deye Cloud and Huawei FusionSolar out of the box.
landingBenefit3Text: Real-time OREE prices and automatic selection of optimal charge and discharge windows.
landingBenefit4Text: Solar generation and DAM price forecasts based on real OREE data.
landingBenefit5Text: Self-hosted FastAPI + React. Full data control and extensibility for your site.
landingContactLead: Leave your contact — we will reach out within one business day.
landingFooterCopy: © Open EMS · 220-km.com · Open energy management system
evTvPageTitle: EV UA Android TV — green energy stats & live charging on your site TV
evTvHeroTitle: Green energy statistics on your charging hub TV
evTvHeroSubtitle: Show live charging status for every station at your location — so drivers see what is happening without opening the app on their phone.
evTvHeroLead: Display real-time stats on how much clean Ukrainian energy powers each session — and encourage drivers to charge when the grid is green, without Russian fossil fuel.
evTvGreenEnergyText: EV UA on Android TV shows live metrics from your hub: solar share, clean kWh delivered today, and CO₂ avoided. Drivers see that their charge runs on energy produced in Ukraine — not imported Russian fossil fuel.
evTvGreenEnergyItem1: Real-time share of solar and other renewable energy at your site
evTvGreenEnergyItem2: Cumulative clean kWh and sessions powered by Ukrainian green energy
evTvGreenEnergyItem3: A visible incentive to charge when clean power is available — less reliance on fossil imports
evTvLiveStatusText: Alongside green-energy stats, the TV lists all chargers at your location with up-to-the-second session state: occupied, free, kW, kWh, and time remaining. Drivers stay informed without picking up their phone.
evTvSetupText: Install Android TV, add the EV UA app, and select your location:
evTvSetupStep1: Buy and mount an Android TV at the charging hub.
evTvSetupStep2: Install EV UA from Google Play or sideload the APK below.
evTvSetupStep3: Pick your location from the in-app list.
evTvDataText: Billing partners supply station data to EV UA. The EV UA server (hosted in the EU) pushes updates to mobile, in-car, and TV clients. Data is protected with end-to-end encryption.
evTvCostText: Hardware and electricity are on you; the EV UA TV software is free on barter terms (logo and app mention on screen). Custom terms and consultation are available.
evTvCostItem3: EV UA software — free on barter terms (logo and app mention on screen).
evTvCostNote: For partnership and custom setup, contact:
